children.heading: .—General Requirements
children.label: a .—General Requirements
children.text: The Secretary of Transportation shall issue guidance to carry out a State block grant program. The guidance shall provide that the Secretary may designate not more than 20 qualified States for each fiscal year to assume administrative responsibility for all airport grant amounts available under this subchapter, except for amounts designated for use at primary airports.
deciding the State has an organization capable of effectively administering a block grant made under this section;
deciding the State uses a satisfactory airport system planning process;
deciding the State uses a programming process acceptable to the Secretary;

finding that the State has agreed to comply with United States Government standard requirements for administering the block grant, including the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.), State and local environmental policy acts, Executive orders, agency regulations and guidance, and other Federal environmental requirements; and42 U.S.C. 4321
finding that the State has agreed to provide the Secretary with program information the Secretary requires.

children.heading: .—Safety and Security Needs and Needs of System
children.label: c .—Safety and Security Needs and Needs of System
children.text: Before deciding whether a planning process is satisfactory or a programming process is acceptable under subsection (b)(2) or (b)(3) of this section, the Secretary shall ensure that the process provides for meeting critical safety and security needs and that the programming process ensures that the needs of the national airport system will be addressed in deciding which projects will receive money from the Government. In carrying out this subsection, the Secretary shall permit a State to use the priority system of the State if such system is not inconsistent with the national priority system.
coordinate and consult with the State;
use the environmental analysis prepared by the State for the project or activity if such analysis is adequate; and
as necessary, consult with the State to describe the supplemental analysis the State must provide to meet applicable Federal requirements.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall provide to each State participating in the block grant program under this section training or updated training materials for the administrative responsibilities assumed by the State under such program at no cost to the State.

children.heading: .—Timing
children.label: 2 .—Timing
children.text: The training or updated training materials provided under paragraph (1) shall be provided at least once during each 2-year period and at any time there is a material change in the program.

children.heading: Roles and Responsibilities of Participating States.—
children.label: f Roles and Responsibilities of Participating States.—

children.heading: .—Airports
children.label: 1 .—Airports
children.text: Unless a State participating in the block grant program under this section expressly agrees in a memorandum of agreement, the Secretary shall not require the State to manage functions and responsibilities for airport actions or projects that do not relate to such program.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Any grant agreement providing funds to be administered under such program shall be consistent with the most recently executed memorandum of agreement between the State and the Federal Aviation Administration.

children.heading: .—Parity
children.label: B .—Parity
children.text: The Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration shall provide parity to participating States and shall only require the same type of information and level of detail for any program agreements and documentation that the Administrator would perform with respect to such action if the State did not participate in the program.

children.label: A
children.text: Grant compliance investigations, determinations, and enforcement.

children.label: B
children.text: Obstruction evaluation and airport airspace analysis, determinations, and enforcement off airport property.

children.label: C
children.text: Non-rulemaking analysis, determinations, and enforcement for proposed improvements on airport properties not associated with this subchapter, or off airport property.

children.label: D
children.text: Land use determinations, compatibility planning, and airport layout plan review and approval (consistent with section 47107(x)) for projects not funded by amounts available under this subchapter.

children.label: E
children.text: Nonaeronautical and special event recommendations and approvals.

children.label: F
children.text: Instrument approach procedure evaluations and determinations.

children.label: G
children.text: Environmental review for projects not funded by amounts available under this subchapter.

children.label: H
children.text: Review and approval of land leases, land releases, changes in on-airport land-use designation, and through-the-fence agreements.
